creator: keith.gaughan@gmail.com
text:
On 02/04/2008, Michael Johnson <ahze@freebsd.org> wrote:

> Nothing looks odd so far, can you send me the output of 'gst-inspect' and
> do you have CPUTYPE set?

gst-inspect produced no output whatsoever, and as I mentioned
previously, I don't have CPUTYPE set. All I have in my make.conf file
are the rules to trigger portconf and a few perl-related knobs that
were automatically put there.

I'm starting to think that this might be a more general problem with
GNOME applications as I've just installed file-roller and that hangs
on startup. Attached is a tarball containing the stderr output from
truss from file-roller and gst-inspect ran as myself and as root, as
well as the relevant contents of /var/db/ports, and my
/usr/local/etc/ports.conf file.

-- 
Keith Gaughan / talideon.com
